etymology.description: 皇(皇) (huáng, "emperor; royal") + 帝(帝) (dì, "emperor; sovereign") = a royal sovereign -> emperor
etymology.interpretation: 皇(皇) (huáng, "emperor; royal") originally depicts the sovereign power and high status of a ruler, often linked to celestial or divine authority in Chinese culture. 帝(帝) (dì, "emperor; sovereign") refers to a supreme ruler, specifically reinforced by its historical connection to the divine rulers in mythology. Combining 皇(皇) (huáng, "emperor; royal") with 帝(帝) (dì, "emperor; sovereign") strengthens the emphasis on supreme authority, thus forming the term for the highest authoritative ruler, the emperor.

examples.chinese: 秦始皇是中国历史上的第一位皇帝(秦始皇是中國歷史上的第一位皇帝)。
examples.pinyin: Qínshǐhuáng shì Zhōngguó lìshǐ shàng de dì yī wèi huángdì.
examples.english: Qin Shi Huang was the first emperor in Chinese history.

examples.chinese: 古代的皇帝统治着辽阔的帝国(古代的皇帝統治著遼闊的帝國)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔdài de huángdì tǒngzhì zhe liáokuò de dìguó.
examples.english: The emperors of ancient times ruled over vast empires.

examples.chinese: 新皇帝登基(新皇帝登基)，全国一片欢腾(全國一片歡騰)。
examples.pinyin: Xīn huángdì dēngjī, quánguó yīpiàn huānténg.
examples.english: With the new emperor's enthronement, the entire nation celebrated with joy.
